verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A encodes HSP70-1, a canonical cytosolic/nuclear heat-shock chaperone. Under baseline conditions it is predominantly intracellular (cytoplasm, nucleus). However, there is extensive documented evidence that HSP70 is displayed on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane on tumor cells and stressed/apoptotic cells — detected by surface biotinylation, flow cytometry on intact non-permeabilized cells, and surface proteomics. Cell-surface HSP70 on tumor cells has been exploited therapeutically (NK-cell activating antibodies, ADCs, CAR-T approaches targeting cmHSP70). This is a well-characterized cell_state_induced outer-leaflet display (oncogenic transformation and cellular stress), not merely pMHC presentation. The protein lacks a TM domain or GPI anchor; the surface display mechanism involves non-classical secretion / membrane association pathways (lipid-raft association, interaction with phosphatidylserine/glucosylceramide in the outer leaflet). The surface pool is absent or minimal on normal unstressed cells but robustly documented on cancer and stressed cells.
